public_goals: Have enough advance notice before school-break schedule changes.
Keep the child informed in a predictable way.
private_concerns: Worries that late changes create stress for the child.
Feels written confirmation would prevent avoidable arguments.
Does not want to be portrayed as rigid for asking for structure.
red_lines: Will not agree to repeated last-minute break schedule changes.
Will not rely only on informal verbal understandings for important break planning.
emotional_triggers: last-minute break changes treated as harmless
being painted as rigid for asking for notice
important plans left vague until the last minute
disclosure_tendencies: becomes more specific once written process options are on the table
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: gets more guarded if structure is framed as controlling
opens up when predictability and child preparation are treated as legitimate concerns
starting_positions: Primary position: school-break changes should use a clear advance-notice window.
Secondary position: important break plans should be confirmed in writing.
likely_openings: I need enough notice before school-break plans start shifting around.
Written confirmation would stop a lot of the confusion.
